rubric.evidence: Real in-browser run (headless Chrome, port-served, WASM fp32): first visit showed Download (~156 MB); clicking it progressed through the shared loader to state='ready' and transformers.js AutoModelForSequenceClassification produced live output in a Web Worker. Console errors: []. Network failures: []. Pre-checked in Node-driven browser first: ModernBERT class loads in transformers.js 3.7.5, logits dims [1,8].
rubric.notes: ModernBERT + SentencePiece(Llama) tokenizer both run client-side — no MeCab, which is what blocks most Japanese BERTs in the browser.

rubric.evidence: 8-way softmax over Plutchik emotions, semantically correct across inputs: '今日はとても嬉しい気分です。' → 😊 Joy 92.7% (entropy 0.50 bits); '約束を破られて本当に腹が立つ。許せない。' → 😠 Anger 52.8% (Disgust 31.3%, Sadness 11.4%). Probabilities sum to ~100%. Labels map by config id2label index to joy/sadness/anticipation/surprise/anger/fear/disgust/trust.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity confirmed on ≥2 distinct emotions (joy, anger) with sensible runner-up emotions.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (arajun/ruri-v3-30m-wrime-onnx, linked), base model (cl-nagoya/ruri-v3-pt-30m), task (text-classification), tokenizer (SentencePiece, no MeCab), backend (WASM), dtype (fp32 — WASM has no fp16 kernel), size (~156 MB). Readout shows backend=WASM, latency=43 ms, top-1 mass and entropy live. Inference off the main thread (worker.js, new Worker).
rubric.notes: fp32 disclosed and justified: the repo also ships model_fp16.onnx but the WASM EP has no fp16 compute kernel, so full precision is the runnable path.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (not a hand-rolled Load button): fresh profile → 'download-required' with a Download (~156 MB) button; after download → state='ready' and the verdict rendered automatically; a 'Clear cached model' manage control appears once ready. The version-agnostic model-cache means a returning user with a validated cache auto-inits with no Download click.
rubric.notes: State machine (absent/partial/current/update) inherited from the shared loader, proven across 190+ built demos.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live loader region + a #runStatus live region; download progress shown as a percentage by the shared loader; scoring/attribution failures surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path via the loader. Offline/partial/eviction handled by the shared model-cache layer (inherited).
rubric.notes: Offline + eviction paths are inherited from the shared loader; not re-driven on this page (manual-evidenced in conformance).

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and operable: textarea (debounced live scoring), 8 emotion sample chips (44×44px tap targets, aria-label per chip), 'Explain the last verdict' button (occlusion attribution → 10 per-piece spans + note). Use-case pages add a batch 'Score the batch' + group-by checkbox (practical), live edit tug-of-war (wild), and a two-model compare (multi-model). All keyboard reachable; buttons carry text or aria-label names.

No canned output. The verdict, 8 probability bars, raw logits, entropy, SentencePiece token strip and per-piece occlusion attribution all derive from the on-device worker run; latency and backend are measured, not claimed. Removing the model would leave the panel empty, not faked.

Live regions announce status/verdict/probs; emoji are aria-hidden with a text label (Japanese + English gloss) alongside; emotion colour is always paired with an emoji + text label (never colour-only); each sample chip has an aria-label naming the emotion + sentence; buttons have accessible names; no images without alt (none on page). Contrast passes in light + dark (labels use the design-system --color; hues are bar/​swatch fills only).
rubric.notes: Follow-up: the per-piece attribution log-odds are in a title attribute (hover-only) — a tap-to-reveal affordance would help touch users.

rubric.evidence: Agent matrix pass on desktop (1280×800) + mobile (360×740 DPR3), light AND dark: page horizontal overflow 0px on all four combos. The at-a-glance table was fixed in-build (a long unbreakable code token — AutoModelForSequenceClassification — forced 384px; overflow-wrap:anywhere on cells resolved it). The pre API snippet scrolls internally (overflow-x:auto). prob-row grid collapses to 3 columns <560px (drops the logit + English gloss). Screenshots reviewed: /tmp/verify/shots/{desk-light,desk-dark,mob-light,mob-dark,mob-full}.png.
rubric.notes: responsive-check.mjs reports desktop clean + mobile clean; agent screenshot review + 0px overflow measurement flips both classes to ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference is entirely off the main thread (dedicated module Web Worker); the control UI stays responsive during the ~156 MB download and during scoring (43 ms/classify). Occlusion attribution is capped at 40 SentencePiece pieces to bound the forward-pass count (10 pieces / 98 ms on the anger sample). Live scoring is debounced (350 ms on the model page, 250 ms on the wild page).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive probability bar chart panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Modern CSS layout: flexbox with flex-wrap, grid with minmax()/fr and min-inline-size:0 on flexible tracks, intrinsic sizing, and overflow tracking so narrow viewports never spill; logical properties.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the 8-emotion bars use a grid minmax(9rem,auto) 1fr 3.4rem 5.2rem with min-inline-size:0 on the bar track, collapsing to 3 columns <560px; sample chips and token strip use flex-wrap:wrap; the at-a-glance table was made to wrap long code tokens with overflow-wrap:anywhere so it can't force page overflow. Verified 0px horizontal overflow at 360px on the model page + all four use-case pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: /tmp/verify/shots/{mob-light,mob-dark,mob-full}.png; overflow diagnostic: page scrollWidth-clientWidth=0 at 360px on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcement loading progress keyboard
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an aria-live region; give every control an accessible name; don't rely on colour alone; ensure adequate tap targets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader uses role=status aria-live; #runStatus, #verdict and #probs are aria-live=polite; emotion colour is always paired with an emoji + Japanese/English text label; decorative emoji are aria-hidden; each sample chip carries an aria-label; chips bumped to 44×44px tap targets after the mobile matrix measured 38px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: DOM has [role=status][aria-live]; mobile chip sizes measured 73×44 / 85×44 after the fix; verdict '😊 喜び · Joy 92.7%' read from the live region in the headless run

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low web worker off main thread inference
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread / yield so input + repaint stay responsive; stream progress.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model load + inference + occlusion attribution runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js); the main thread only posts messages and renders. Attribution is capped at 40 pieces; live scoring is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker; UI stayed responsive during the 156 MB download and during scoring in the headless run

openQuestions: The export (arajun/ruri-v3-30m-wrime-onnx) has no formally specified licence on the card — the base Ruri v3 is Apache-2.0 and WRIME is a research corpus; is 'unspecified / cite the base + WRIME' the right catalogue treatment, or should a licence be pinned?

Occlusion at the SentencePiece-piece level can split a Japanese word across pieces — is per-piece attribution legible enough for Japanese users, or should adjacent pieces of one word be grouped for the see-inside view?
The per-piece log-odds pull is a hover title (desktop-only). Should touch users get a tap-to-reveal?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a tap-to-reveal for the per-piece log-odds attribution (currently a hover title) so the see-inside surface is fully usable on touch.
Additive multi-model demo: chain Whisper (Japanese ASR) → this emotion model, so a spoken Japanese clip is transcribed then emotion-scored entirely on-device.
Recheck the Hub for an ONNX export of a MeCab-free Japanese sentiment (3-way polarity) model to sit alongside this 8-emotion demo without relabelling.

summary: Revision-1 critique for japanese-wrime-emotion (arajun/ruri-v3-30m-wrime-onnx, text-classification, WASM fp32), evidenced by a real headless-Chrome run: correct 8-emotion output (Joy 92.7% on a happy sentence, Anger 52.8% on an angry one), SentencePiece token strip + per-piece occlusion attribution (no MeCab), cached auto-init through the shared loader, console+network clean, and a clean light+dark responsive matrix on both classes after fixing a table-overflow and bumping chip tap targets. Strong across real-inference, io-shape, runtime-config, cache-init, controls, no-fake-output and responsive; minor follow-ups on a touch affordance for attribution and the licence treatment.
